characters.name: Addison Cobblepot
characters.description: Addison CobblepotGeneral InformationReal name:Addison CobblepotFirst Appearance:Batman Vol 3 #126(October, 2022)Created by:Chip Zdarsky and Belén OrtegaAddison Cobblepotwas the one ofPenguin Twinsdaughter of Oswald Cobblepot, the crimelord also known asPenguin. (October, 2022) Addison andAidenwere born to the same mother, but the twins apparently attempted to kill their half-siblings after the "death" of their father to collect their inheritance. Their plan did not work as intended, and all she and his brother inherited was theIceberg Loungeand related holdings. Mobility Issue: She apparently inherited her father's leg problems.

characters.name: Alfred Beagle
characters.description: Alfred BeagleGeneral InformationReal name:Alfred BeagleFirst Appearance:Batman#16(April–May 1943)Created by:Don C. Cameron(writer),Jerry Robinson(artist)Affiliations:Bat-FamilyAbilities:Medical training Alfred Beaglewas born as the son of Jarvis Beagle, a retired military agent who had proved to be less than apt as his father though a competent military officer.[citation needed]Following the service of the First World War, Beagle followed the deathbed request of his father who used to be a butler for the Wayne family. Alfred sought out Wayne to fulfill his father's last request and upon arriving toGotham City, Alfred introduced himself toBruce WayneandDick GraysonatWayne Manorand insisted on becoming their butler.[1] Originally the pair did not want to jeopardize their secret identities with a servant in the house, they did not reject him outright. However as fate would have it, Beagle stumbled onto theBatcaveand deduced his master's secret identities. Alfred would later rescue the dynamic duo from a trap and aided them to capture the criminals responsible. Alfred's sense of responsibility and his knowledge of their secret identities convinced Bruce Wayne that he could be depended on to keep their secrets and manage the day to day operations of Wayne Manor. Since then, Alfred became Bruce Wayne's butler and Batman's assistant.[1]
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

However as fate would have it, Beagle stumbled onto theBatcaveand deduced his master's secret identities. Alfred would later rescue the dynamic duo from a trap and aided them to capture the criminals responsible. characters.name: Azrael
characters.description: Azraelis a name given to the champions of the amoral Catholic extremist guild, theOrder of St. Dumas.  Azrael acts as the deranged agent of the Order, often serving as an ally and enemy toBatmandue to his violence, diluted moral-system, and homicidal tendencies. See:Jean-Paul Valley The first Azrael in Batman comics wasJean-Paul Valley, a bottle-baby who was genetically engineered with animal DNA by his father, the previous Azrael.  Since his childhood, Jean-Paul was psychologically conditioned by his father and the Order to become his father's successor.  When he was a young man, his father died and sent him off to Europe to directly train under the Order.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: B-Devil
characters.description: B-DevilGeneral InformationReal name:unknownFirst Appearance:Batman: Gotham KnightPortrayed by:Scott Menville(voice)B-Devilis one of the teens living in Gotham inBatman: Gotham Knight. B-Devil was a teenager inGotham Citywho would occasionally meet with his friends at a Gotham City skatepark. One day, when at the skatepark, his friends,Porkchop,Meesh, andDandertell him stories of meeting theBatman, with each story having Batman possessing different supernatural abilities. As the teens tell their story, Batman had pursued theMan in Blackback to the skatepark where B-Devil and his friends were. With the help of Batman, B-Devil takes down the Man in Black, and sees Batman as he truly is, a human warrior fit with aBatsuit.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Bagley
characters.description: BagleyGeneral InformationFirst Appearance:The BatmanAffiliations:Arkham AsylumPortrayed by:Henry Gibson Bagleywas the former chief of psychiatry atArkham Asylum. He appeared inTopsy Turvy, where he was on a phone call with the press. He kept bragging about his achievements and how he has been invited to the Gotham Palace Theater to a banquet of honor.

characters.name: Ms. Bambi
characters.description: Ms. BambiGeneral InformationReal name:BambiPortrayed by:Arleen SorkinAppearances:Batman: Mask of the PhantasmThe New Batman AdventuresMs. Bambiwas a female socialite based inGotham City, who pursuedBruce Wayneas a potential suitor. Bambi attended a gathering atWayne Manorwhere Bruce Wayne stood amidst his suitors. Upon the comment made that Bruce was nervous of the "M" word (marriage), Bambi attempted to add some wit onto the topic of whether he was afraid of the "I" word, which she revealed to be "ingagement" (which is actually spelled "engagement"). After one of them threw a glass of wine into Bruce's face for forgetting her phone number from a previous relationship, Bruce left all of them to reflect upon his past love in his study.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Batman of Zur-En-Arrh
characters.actor_name: Kevin Conroy-Batman: The Brave and the Bold
characters.description: Batman of Zur-En-ArrhGeneral InformationReal name:TlanoFirst Appearance:Batman#113(August 1958)Created by:Bill FingerAffiliations:Bat-FamilyDaily Planet (Zur-En-Arrh)Abilities:Genius-level intelligenceMaster detectiveMaster escapologistPeak human physical conditionMaster martial artistAccess to high tech equipmentPortrayed by:Kevin Conroy-Batman: The Brave and the BoldTheBatman of Zur-En-Arrhis an alternate, alien form of the Batman moniker.  Originally introduced as the alien scientistTlano, later comics revealed that the alien is actually a violent alternate-personality ofBruce Wayne. One night, Bruce Wayne finds himself in a daze. He dresses as Batman and takes off in theBatplanewhile remaining unclear of his own actions. He soon finds that he has been teleported to another planet, Zur-En-Arrh. There he meets a scientist named Tlano who has been monitoring his activities on Earth, and has decided to become a version of Batman for his own planet. On this planet, the Batman of Earth has enhanced abilities due to the different elements of the alien planet. The two Batmen join forces to defeat giant invading robots piloted by an unidentified alien race. After the robots are destroyed, the alien Batman gives Batman/Bruce Wayne his Bat-Radia device as a keepsake, and returns him to Earth. Batman of Zur-En-Arrh
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Tim Drake (Titans Tomorrow)
characters.description: Tim Drake as BatmanGeneral InformationReal name:Timothy Jackson DrakeFirst Appearance:Teen Titans/Legion Special #1Created by:Geoff Johns, Mike McKone and Marlo AlquizaAffiliations:Teen Titans,Batman familyAbilities:Peak physical ability and mental prowessA possible future where Drake becomesBatman This version ofTim Drakehails from an alternate future reality where as an adult he took on the mantle of the Batman - something that his younger counterpart swore he would never become. This alternate timeline branched from the regular New Earth timeline shortly following an adventure wherein the Teen Titans traveled to the 31st Century to assist the Legion of Super-Heroes against the Fatal Five-Hundred. While trying to return home, they accidentally arrived at a point in time ten years into their own future. They met older versions of themselves who had adopted a more violent, fascist approach towards crime prevention. Upon returning to their own era however, these Teen Titans swore that this future would never come to pass, and decided to disband rather than stay together. What none of them realized however, was that the break-up of the Teen Titans was actually the catalyst that created the branched timeline to begin with. In the Titans Tomorrow era, the world changed dramatically as a result of a great "Crisis". Though little information regarding the specifics of this Crisis has yet to be revealed, One thing that is known is that Tim's predecessorBruce Waynewas killed during this Crisis and his legacy was passed down to Tim. As Tim tells it, the world grew more dark and cold following the Crisis, and the surviving heroes had to adapt to the changing times. The nation was split into two factions, East and West. The former Teen Titans regrouped and established a base of operations at their previous Titans Tower HQ in San Francisco. A renegade group of Titans re-established themselves in New York City and became known as Titans East. Tim and his colleagues within the Titans adapted a zero-tolerance approach towards criminals.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Batman (Brane Taylor)
characters.description: Batman 3000General InformationReal name:Brane TaylorFirst Appearance:Batman #26 (December, 1944)Created by:Bill FingerDick SprangCharles ParisBrane Taylor(originally simply known asBrane) is a possible future hero who becameBatman(referred to in comics asBatman 3000) after watching some archived footage of the Dynamic Duo in action.  In the 31st century a future relative of Bruce Wayne, who had no prior knowledge of their connection, had take the mantle of 'TheBatman'. It was around this time, the 31st century, that Saturn's army of Fura has conquered much ofEarthduring the pre-crisis. After a long 'guerrilla' warfare battle against the Saturnian forces, Batman (Brane Taylor) and his nephew (Ricky), both rendered the Saturn army ofFurauseless.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.description: Kate KaneGeneral InformationReal name:Katherine KaneFirst Appearance:52#11Created by:Bob KaneSheldon MoldoffAffiliations:Bat-FamilyJustice LeagueAbilities:Trained martial artistAccess to high tech equipmentPortrayed by:Yvonne Strahovski(voice)Ruby RoseWallis DayTara Strong(voice)Stephanie Beartriz(voice)Katherine "Kate" Kaneis the secondBatwoman. characters.name: Bekka
characters.description: BekkaGeneral InformationReal name:BekkaFirst Appearance:DC Graphic Novel #4Created by:Jack KirbyAffiliations:New Gods Bekkais the daughter of Himon, one of the New Gods whose achievements include invention of the sentient Mother Box computers and discovery of the "x-element" that fueled his co-creation of the teleporting Boom Tubes alongside fellow scientist Metron. For many years, Bekka lived with her father in secret on Apokolips as Himon organized an underground uprising against Darkseid's tyrannical rule. Hunted for his role in this planned insurgence, Himon took extra care to shield Bekka from Darkseid's notice. Himon never imagined that Bekka would fall in love with Orion, Darkseid's own son, when he stumbled into their lives. After healing the wounded Dog of War, Himon and Bekka aided Orion in freeing his mother Tigra from imprisonment. For this act, Himon was seemingly slain by the dread lord of Apokolips. Bekka then accompanied Orion and Tigra to New Genesis, where her love continued to soothe the savagery that lurks within the Dog of War. During the "Torment" incident, she andBatmanhelped Superman escape from the planet Tartarus, where Superman is being held prisoner by Desaad. During the rescue mission, she and Batman developed an attraction and her affection caused Batman to reflect on the lack of love he has forced himself to endure. Bekka likened her spell of desire to a siren's call but said that it affects her as well. In the end, however, she left with Orion when he came to get her. In her home staying with Orion, while thinking that she will forget Batman, some unknown person approached her, and she is then apparently killed as part of the Death of the New Gods.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Mr. Bloom
characters.description: Mr. BloomGeneral InformationReal name:UnknownFirst Appearance:Batman Vol 2 #43 (October, 2015)Created by:Scott Snyder(writer)Greg Capullo(artist)Abilities:ClawsSuperhuman agilityElasticitySuperhuman strength Mister Bloomwas a metahuman criminal introduced as the nemesis ofJim Gordon's incarnation of Batman followingBruce Wayne's supposed death. Mister Bloom is a twisted meta-human who sells black-market, "Seeds" which can grant superpowers with the mission of destroying Gotham so it can be rebuilt from the ashes as a less corrupt society. Technically the original Mr. Bloom was a scientist with good-intentions namedDaryl Gutierrezwho after the events of Gotham's Zero Year began work on a breed of high-tech nanotech reactors he called "seeds" which gave their users randomized superpowers in hopes of making more superheroes like Batman. Daryl gave up the alias of Bloom after his cousin Pete Duggio used a seed to turn into a winged vigilante, only to be gunned down by a scared police-officer.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Bluebird
characters.description: BluebirdGeneral InformationReal name:Harper RowFirst Appearance:Batman#7(March, 2012)Created by:Scott SnyderGreg CapulloAffiliations:BatmanAbilities:GadgetryHarper Rowgrew up in the Narrows ofGotham Citywith her younger brother,Cullen. They both had a rough life due to abuse, abandonment and the lack of money. She later became the vigilanteBluebird. Harper's father had a habit of breaking things, then would disappear for stretches of time. During this time Harper would fix the things her father broke. Harper claims that her earliest memories are of watching the building super strip and graft wires, and fix things that seemed beyond repair. Harper soon developed a talent for fixing things herself. Her relationship with her father is stated to have been abusive, as she lists herself and her brother among the things he would break. Eventually Harper applied for emancipation. After achieving this, she moved out, taking her brotherCullenwith her, and applied for a job with the city electrical engineer, and gets a job doing maintenance on the city's electrical grid. Harper and Cullen moved into the narrows, and broke contact with their father. Unfortunately some of the local bullies began targeting Cullen, seemingly because of his sexual orientation.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Bluebird
characters.description: BluebirdGeneral InformationReal name:Harper RowFirst Appearance:Batman#7(March, 2012)Created by:Scott SnyderGreg CapulloAffiliations:BatmanAbilities:GadgetryHarper Rowgrew up in the Narrows ofGotham Citywith her younger brother,Cullen. They both had a rough life due to abuse, abandonment and the lack of money. She later became the vigilanteBluebird. Harper's father had a habit of breaking things, then would disappear for stretches of time. During this time Harper would fix the things her father broke. characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Sasha Bordeaux
characters.description: Sasha BordeauxGeneral InformationReal name:Sasha BordeauxFirst Appearance:Detective Comics#751Created by:Greg RuckaShawn MartinbroughAffiliations:Bat-FamilyCheckmateAbilities:Professional BodyguardSasha Bordeauxis a former ally of bothBruce Wayneand theBat-Familyshe later became a government agent of Checkmate. Sasha Bordeaux is hired as Bruce Wayne's bodyguard. She is assigned by Wayne Enterprise's second in command, Lucius Fox, against Wayne's wishes. Wayne at first tries to avoid her, but she keeps doing her job. She eventually becomes suspicious and did some searching, where she discovers that Bruce is secretly Batman. Knowing he couldn't fire her because she knew his secret identity, Batman trains her to be his apprentice. After weeks of training, she is fit enough to go out with him on patrol in a suit, though he still treats her as a total stranger both as Batman and as Bruce Wayne. During this time, she falls in love with him, even as he insists on having her around while he dated other women.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Stephanie Brown
characters.description: The SpoilerGeneral InformationReal name:Stephanie BrownFirst Appearance:Detective Comics#647 (August, 1992)Created by:Chuck DixonTom LyleAffiliations:Bat-FamilyFormerly:Birds of PreyAbilities:Stealth AbilitiesStephanie Brownis theSpoiler. She also briefly served as aRobinfor theBat-Familyand later assumed the mantle ofBatgirl. Stephanie  was born the daughter of theCluemaster, one ofGotham City's third-rate villains. Stephanie's father spent most of her childhood in prison or away from the family, and though he claimed to be "rehabilitated upon his return to Gotham", Stephanie was furious to discover that he was actually returning to crime without his need to leave clues behind. She decided something needed to be done. Stephanie tailored a costume for herself, and called herself the Spoiler. She knew where her father was hiding out, found out his plans, and left clues so that the police andBatmancould stop him.Robin IIItracked her down, and she joined in on the capturing of Cluemaster. She also became attracted to Robin, and it annoyed her that he knew her identity but she did not know his. Stephanie as the Spoiler.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Batman
characters.description: BatmanGeneral InformationReal name:Bruce Thomas WayneFirst Appearance:Detective Comics#27(May, 1939)Created by:Bill Finger(writer)Bob Kane(artist)Affiliations:See:RelationshipsAbilities:Genius-level intelligenceMaster detectiveMaster escapologistPeak human physical conditionMaster martial artistAccess to high tech equipmentPortrayed by:See:In Other Media Batmanis a crimefighter operating inGotham City, he serves as its protector, using the symbol of abatto strike fear into the hearts of criminals. Unlike other superheroes, Batman is often depicted to lack any "superpowers", instead using lifelong training and equipment to fight crime. His secret identity isBruce Wayne, a rich playboy and philanthropist who swore to fight crime after witnessing his parents' brutal murder. He has served as a member of various teams, including theJustice League, theJustice Society of America, and theOutsiders. Batman is also the founder and leader of various teams, including theBat-Family,Batmen of All NationsandBatman Incorporated. He also goes by numerous nicknames, including "The Dark Knight", "the Caped Crusader", "World's Greatest Detective" and the "Defender of Gotham". Co-created by artistBob Kaneand writerBill Finger, Batman made his first appearance inDetective Comics#27(May, 1939). He quickly became a popular character soon after his introduction and eventually gainedhis own comic publicationa year after his debut. As the decades wore on, differing takes on the character emerged. The late 1960s Batman television series utilized a camp aesthetic associated with the character for years after the show ended. Various creators worked to return the character to his dark roots, culminating inFrank Miller's1986Dark Knightseries. It and the success of directorTim Burton's 1989 Batman motion picture helped reignite popular interest in the character. A cultural icon, Batman is often regarded as one of the most renowned and popular superheroes of all time. He has been licensed and adapted into various media, including live-action television and film, diverse forms of animation, and video games.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Bruno
characters.description: BrunoGeneral InformationFirst Appearance:Dark Knight#3 -Hunt the Dark KnightCreated by:Frank MillerAffiliations:The JokerAbnerAbilities:Unarmed CombatPortrayed by:Tress MacNeille(voice)Brunois a Neo-Nazi woman and ruthless gang leader inGotham City, originating from the possible future storyDark Knightand its elseworld prequels. One ofBatman's more obscure enemies, nowadays she is arguably best remembered for the distinctive pair of red swastika pasties on her exposed breasts and glutes. Bruno was a muscular nazi criminal active in Gotham City.  During Batman's prime years around the 1950s-1960s, Bruno worked as a henchwoman for the Joker.  She assisted Joker in disposing of the corpse belonging to attorney Donna Gugina whom he murdered while they had sex at a hotel. Bruno continued her criminal career after Batman and theJustice Leaguewere forced by the government into retirement, leading a small gang of robbers.  Her, "Boyfriend" (possibly referring to the Joker) was incarcerated within Arkham, leaving her to lead her own gang, supposedly to fund body surgeries.  Her gang would include Rob and Don, two former members of theMutants gangaftertheir leaderwas beaten by a resurged Batman.
characters.media_title: Batman: The Brave and the Bold

characters.name: Bryce Napier (White Knight)
characters.description: Bryce NapierGeneral InformationReal name:Bryce NapierFirst Appearance:Batman: White Knight apresenta Harley Quinn #2 (2020)Bryce Napier, one of the twin offspring of Harley Quinn, Bryce, along with his twin sisterJackie, are also the children ofJack Napier, widely recognized as the infamous Joker. Bryce is a pre-adolescent boy, blonde like his mother, with a slightly pointed nose like his father, and he has both purple eyes inherited from one of his father's heterochromatic eyes. Bryce is an exceptionally well-mannered and intellectually gifted young boy, surpassing expectations in academic achievements at one of Gotham City's schools. He stands in stark contrast to his sister, Jackie, exhibiting a calm, shy, reserved, nature and a Cautious sense of judgment. Nevertheless, Bryce maintains a strong bond with his sister, remaining ever vigilant and deeply concerned about her well-being, knowing that she has a tendency to find herself in problematic situations when he is not by her side. Bryce is an exceptionally well-mannered and intellectually gifted young boy, surpassing expectations in academic achievements at one of Gotham City's schools. He stands in stark contrast to his sister, Jackie, exhibiting a calm, shy, reserved, nature and a Cautious sense of judgment. Nevertheless, Bryce maintains a strong bond with his sister, remaining ever vigilant and deeply concerned about her well-being, knowing that she has a tendency to find herself in problematic situations when he is not by her side. Both siblings handle their father's absence in their own way. Bryce's approach is more silent, engaging in discussions with strangers on internet forums about their father's criminal past. Bryce had done his own research into the Joker's history, fearing that his own mental state would undergo a similar change to his father's.
